            "📥 Fetching Tesla (TSLA) historical market data...\n",
            "\n",
            "📈 Market Data Loaded Successfully!\n",
            "Total trading days tracked: 506\n",
            "Columns available: ['Date', 'AAPL.Open', 'AAPL.High', 'AAPL.Low', 'AAPL.Close', 'AAPL.Volume', 'AAPL.Adjusted', 'dn', 'mavg', 'up', 'direction']\n"
          ]
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"📥 Fetching Tesla (TSLA) historical market data...\")\n",
        "# Secure, pre-formatted historical stock data mirror\n",
        "stock_url = \"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datasets/finance-vix/master/data/vix-daily.csv\"\n",
        "# Let's pull a beautifully curated tech stock history dataset instead to keep it clean and relevant:\n",
        "tech_url = \"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/plotly/datasets/master/finance-charts-apple.csv\"\n",
        "\n",
        "# Load the data\n",
        "df_stock = pd.read_csv(tech_url)\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"\\n📈 Market Data Loaded Successfully!\")\n",
        "print(f\"Total trading days tracked: {df_stock.shape[0]}\")\n",
        "print(f\"Columns available: {list(df_stock.columns)}\")\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"Calculating 20-Day Simple Moving Average (SMA)...\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# Calculate the rolling average of the closing price over a 20-day window\n",
        "df_stock['SMA_20'] = df_stock['AAPL.Close'].rolling(window=20).mean()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Display the date, the actual close price, and our new SMA tool for the last 10 records\n",
        "print(\"\\n📋 --- RECENT CLOSING PRICES vs. 20-DAY SMA --- 📋\")\n",
        "print(df_stock[['Date', 'AAPL.Close', 'SMA_20']].tail(10))\n"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "MfOjxxFaYauc",
        "outputId": "c3ce0347-db63-454d-fc5e-825f6e88d528"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Calculating 20-Day Simple Moving Average (SMA)...\n",
            "\n",
            "📋 --- RECENT CLOSING PRICES vs. 20-DAY SMA --- 📋\n",
            "           Date  AAPL.Close      SMA_20\n",
            "496  2017-02-03  129.080002  121.449000\n",
            "497  2017-02-06  130.289993  122.067999\n",
            "498  2017-02-07  131.529999  122.694999\n",
            "499  2017-02-08  132.039993  123.341499\n",
            "500  2017-02-09  132.419998  123.974999\n",
            "501  2017-02-10  132.119995  124.618499\n",
            "502  2017-02-13  133.289993  125.330998\n",
            "503  2017-02-14  135.020004  126.081998\n",
            "504  2017-02-15  135.509995  126.857998\n",
            "505  2017-02-16  135.350006  127.636499\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"Engineering Trading Bot Logic...\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. Calculate the fast 5-day moving average\n",
        "df_stock['SMA_5'] = df_stock['AAPL.Close'].rolling(window=5).mean()\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. Program the Buy/Sell signals\n",
        "# If SMA_5 is greater than SMA_20, output 'BUY'. Otherwise, output 'SELL'.\n",
        "df_stock['Signal'] = np.where(df_stock['SMA_5'] > df_stock['SMA_20'], 'BUY', 'SELL')\n",
        "\n",
        "# 3. Track the exact day the signal CHANGES (so we don't buy/sell continuously)\n",
        "df_stock['Action'] = df_stock['Signal'].shift(1)\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"\\n🤖 --- TRADING BOT SIGNALS GENERATED --- 📋\")\n",
        "# Display the final 15 days to see what the bot is currently recommending\n",
        "print(df_stock[['Date', 'AAPL.Close', 'SMA_5', 'SMA_20', 'Signal']].tail(15))\n"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "KkUE4KbGYmEW",
        "outputId": "c057bc64-0426-4c96-efec-266b15f2c752"
      },

    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "\n",
        "# We will plot the last 100 days of trading data so the lines are easy to see on a phone\n",
        "chart_data = df_stock.tail(100)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. Plot the actual closing price and both moving averages\n",
        "plt.plot(chart_data['Date'], chart_data['AAPL.Close'], label='Actual Close Price', color='dodgerblue', linewidth=2)\n",
        "plt.plot(chart_data['Date'], chart_data['SMA_5'], label='5-Day SMA (Fast)', color='orange', linestyle='--')\n",
        "plt.plot(chart_data['Date'], chart_data['SMA_20'], label='20-Day SMA (Slow)', color='crimson', linewidth=2)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. Add titles and style\n",
        "plt.title('Algorithmic Trading Strategy: Crossover Trends')\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Date')\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Stock Price ($)')\n",
        "plt.xticks(chart_data['Date'].iloc[::20], rotation=45) # Show date tags every 20 days so they don't overlap\n",
        "plt.legend(loc='upper left')\n",
        "plt.grid(True, linestyle=':', alpha=0.6)\n",
        "\n",
        "# 3. Fit perfectly on mobile screens\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()\n"
      ],
